1	# MawarBiru — Task List
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	MawarBiru is a luxury wedding and event venue website built as a Next.js monolith with MySQL, Prisma, and Tailwind CSS. Public visitors browse packages, book consultation appointments (temujanji), and check venue availability via a colour-coded calendar. A single business owner manages everything through an admin panel.
6	
7	**Rigor split:** Client-facing pages (phases 3-4) receive full treatment — every interactive state, responsive breakpoint, accessibility requirement, edge case, and performance consideration is specified. Admin modules (phase 6) use standard rigor — functional correctness and basic usability without exhaustive state/a11y enumeration.
8	
9	## Dependency Overview
10	
11	```
12	Phase 1: Foundation
13	  └── Phase 2: Design System (depends on 1.1-1.4)
14	        └── Phase 3: Client Layout (depends on 2.1-2.5)
15	              └── Phase 4: Client Feature Slices (depends on 3.1-3.3)
16	                    └── Phase 5: Cross-cutting Concerns (depends on 2.x, 4.x)
17	                          └── Phase 6: Admin Surface (depends on 1.x, 2.x)
18	                                └── Phase 7: Quality & Polish (depends on all above)
19	                                      └── Phase 8: Release Prep (depends on all above)
20	```
21	
22	Phase 1 establishes the repo, database, and auth. Phase 2 builds the reusable design tokens and components. Phase 3 assembles the public-facing shell (nav, footer, layout). Phase 4 implements the four client pages with full interactivity. Phase 5 adds cross-cutting client concerns. Phase 6 builds the admin CRUD surface. Phase 7 polishes and audits. Phase 8 ships.
23	
24	## File Index
25	
26	| File | Phase | Surface | Tasks |
27	|------|-------|---------|-------|
28	| `01-foundation.md` | 1 — Foundation | Shared | 1.1–1.6 |
29	| `02-design-system.md` | 2 — Design System | Client | 2.1–2.5 |
30	| `03-client-layout.md` | 3 — Client Layout | Client | 3.1–3.3 |
31	| `04-client-homepage.md` | 4.1 — Homepage | Client | 4.1 |
32	| `04-client-pakej.md` | 4.2 — Pakej Page | Client | 4.2 |
33	| `04-client-temujanji.md` | 4.3, 4.5 — Temujanji | Client | 4.3, 4.5 |
34	| `04-client-kalendar.md` | 4.4, 4.6 — Kalendar | Client | 4.4, 4.6 |
35	| `05-cross-cutting.md` | 5 — Cross-cutting | Client | 5.1–5.7 |
36	| `06-admin-surface.md` | 6 — Admin Surface | Admin | 6.1–6.9 |

38	| `08-release-prep.md` | 8 — Release Prep | Shared | 8.1–8.6 |

1	# Phase 1 — Foundation
2	
3	---
4	
5	## Task 1.1: Initialise Next.js Project and Repository
6	
7	**Description:** Scaffold a new Next.js 14+ (App Router) project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and ESLint. Initialise git, configure `.gitignore`, and set up the basic folder structure.
8	
9	**Acceptance criteria:**
10	- [ ] `npm run dev` starts the dev server without errors
11	- [ ] TypeScript strict mode enabled in `tsconfig.json`
12	- [ ] Tailwind CSS configured with empty `theme.extend` ready for tokens
13	- [ ] ESLint runs clean on the scaffolded code
14	- [ ] Folder structure follows `app/(client)/`, `app/(admin)/`, `lib/`, `components/ui/`, `components/client/`, `components/admin/`, `prisma/`
15	- [ ] `.gitignore` excludes `node_modules`, `.next`, `.env*`, `prisma/*.db`
16	- [ ] Git repository initialised with initial commit
17	
18	**Technical notes:**
19	- Use `create-next-app` with `--typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir=false`
20	- Add `prettier` and `prettier-plugin-tailwindcss` for consistent formatting
21	- Configure path aliases: `@/` maps to project root
22	
23	**Dependencies:** None
24	
25	**Estimate:** S
26	
27	**Out of scope:** Database setup, authentication, design tokens, deployment configuration.
28	
29	---
30	
31	## Task 1.2: Configure Prisma with MySQL and Define Schema
32	
33	**Description:** Install Prisma, configure the MySQL datasource via environment variable, and define the complete data model: User, Pelanggan, SlotTemujanji, Temujanji, SlotTempahan, Tempahan, Pakej, PakejTier, PakejSection.
34	
35	**Acceptance criteria:**
36	- [ ] `prisma/schema.prisma` contains all 9 models with correct fields, types, and relations
37	- [ ] `User` model has `id`, `email`, `passwordHash`, `nama`, `createdAt`
38	- [ ] `Pelanggan` has `id`, `nama`, `noTelefon`, `nota`, `createdAt`
39	- [ ] `SlotTemujanji` has `id`, `tarikh` (DateTime date-only), `masaMula` (String HH:mm), `masaTamat` (String HH:mm), `status` (enum: TERSEDIA, DITEMPAH, DIBATALKAN), `createdAt`
40	- [ ] `Temujanji` has `id`, `pelangganId`, `slotTemujanjiId`, `nota`, `createdAt` — unique constraint on `slotTemujanjiId`
41	- [ ] `SlotTempahan` has `id`, `tarikh` (DateTime date-only), `status` (enum: TERSEDIA, DITEMPAH, DIBATALKAN), `createdAt`
42	- [ ] `Tempahan` has `id`, `namaTempahan`, `slotTempahanId`, `pakejId`, `pelangganId`, `nota`, `createdAt` — no kategori field
43	- [ ] `Pakej` has `id`, `namaPakej`, `warna` (String, hex colour), `gambar` (String, nullable), `createdAt`
44	- [ ] `PakejTier` has `id`, `pakejId`, `namaTier`, `bilanganPax`, `harga` (Decimal), `createdAt`
45	- [ ] `PakejSection` has `id`, `pakejId`, `tajuk`, `items` (Json array of strings), `createdAt`
46	- [ ] All relations use `@relation` with `onDelete` behaviour specified
47	- [ ] `npx prisma validate` passes
48	- [ ] `npx prisma generate` succeeds
49	- [ ] Migration runs cleanly against a fresh MySQL database
50	- [ ] `.env.example` documents `DATABASE_URL` format without real credentials
51	
52	**Technical notes:**
53	- Use `@db.Date` for date-only fields if supported, otherwise store as `DateTime` and truncate in application code
54	- `masaMula`/`masaTamat` stored as `String` ("10:00", "12:00") — simpler than DateTime for 2-hour display slots
55	- `harga` uses `Decimal` with `@db.Decimal(10, 2)` for currency precision
56	- `PakejSection.items` is a JSON array — Prisma supports `Json` type on MySQL
57	- Seed script is a separate task (1.3)
58	
59	**Dependencies:** 1.1
60	
61	**Estimate:** M
62	
63	**Out of scope:** Seed data, API routes, Prisma Client singleton setup.
64	
65	---
66	
67	## Task 1.3: Create Prisma Client Singleton and Seed Script
68	
69	**Description:** Set up a global Prisma Client singleton for Next.js (avoiding multiple instances in development) and write a seed script that populates the database with the single admin user and the 3 base Pakej records with their tiers and sections.
70	
71	**Acceptance criteria:**
72	- [ ] `lib/db.ts` exports a singleton `prisma` instance using the global cache pattern
73	- [ ] `prisma/seed.ts` creates 1 admin User with hashed password from env var `ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH`
74	- [ ] Seed creates Pakej "Night Wedding" with tiers: 300 PAX / RM15,500, 500 PAX / RM18,500, 800 PAX / RM21,500
75	- [ ] Seed creates Pakej "Pakej Wedding" with tiers: Seroja 500 PAX / RM20,500, Melur 800 PAX / RM23,500, Teratai 1000 PAX / RM25,500
76	- [ ] Seed creates Pakej "Event Package" with tiers: Breakfast RM10/pax, Lunch RM23/pax, Hi-Tea RM23/pax, Dinner RM23/pax, Hall Rental RM2,500 (3hrs) / RM4,000 (5hrs)
77	- [ ] Each Pakej has sections with menu items (Menu Kenduri, Menu Kampung, Termasuk, Percuma)
78	- [ ] Each Pakej has a default `warna` hex value assigned
79	- [ ] Seed is idempotent — running twice does not create duplicates (upsert pattern)
80	- [ ] `npx prisma db seed` runs without errors
81	- [ ] `package.json` has `prisma.seed` script configured
82	
83	**Technical notes:**
84	- Prisma Client singleton pattern: attach to `globalThis` in development, plain instance in production
85	- Use `bcrypt` or `bcryptjs` to hash the admin password; seed reads from `ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH` env var to avoid plaintext in code
86	- Upsert on unique fields (`email` for User, `namaPakej` for Pakej)
87	
88	**Dependencies:** 1.2
89	
90	**Estimate:** S
91	
92	**Out of scope:** API routes, admin UI, dynamic package content.
93	
94	---
95	
96	## Task 1.4: Set Up NextAuth.js for Admin Authentication
97	
98	**Description:** Configure NextAuth.js with a Credentials provider for single-admin login. Only the admin panel routes need protection; all client-facing pages are public.
99	
100	**Acceptance criteria:**
101	- [ ] NextAuth.js configured in `app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts`
102	- [ ] Credentials provider authenticates against the User table (email + password via bcrypt compare)
103	- [ ] Session strategy is JWT (no database sessions needed for single user)
104	- [ ] Session includes `user.id` and `user.nama`
105	- [ ] `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` and `NEXTAUTH_URL` documented in `.env.example`
106	- [ ] `lib/auth.ts` exports `authOptions` and a `getServerSession` helper
107	- [ ] Middleware in `middleware.ts` protects all `/admin/*` routes — redirects to `/admin/login` if unauthenticated
108	- [ ] `/admin/login` route is excluded from middleware protection
109	- [ ] Client-facing routes (`/`, `/temujanji`, `/kalendar`, `/pakej`) are never protected
110	- [ ] Invalid credentials return a generic "E-mel atau kata laluan tidak sah" error (no info leakage)
111	- [ ] Session expiry set to 24 hours
112	
113	**Technical notes:**
114	- Use `next-auth@5` (Auth.js v5) if stable, otherwise `next-auth@4` with App Router adapter
115	- Middleware uses `matcher` config to target only `/admin/:path*` excluding `/admin/login`
116	- All auth error messages in Bahasa Malaysia
117	- No registration flow — single admin seeded in 1.3
118	
119	**Dependencies:** 1.3
120	
121	**Estimate:** M
122	
123	**Out of scope:** Admin login page UI (covered in admin phase), password reset, multi-user roles, OAuth providers.
124	
125	---
126	
127	## Task 1.5: Configure Environment Variables and Validation
128	
129	**Description:** Create a runtime environment validation module that fails fast at startup if required variables are missing.
130	
131	**Acceptance criteria:**
132	- [ ] `lib/env.ts` exports a validated env object using Zod schema
133	- [ ] Required variables: `DATABASE_URL`, `NEXTAUTH_SECRET`, `NEXTAUTH_URL`, `ADMIN_EMAIL`
134	- [ ] Optional variables: `WHATSAPP_NUMBER` (defaults to empty string)
135	- [ ] Validation runs at import time — server crashes immediately with clear error if a required var is missing
136	- [ ] `.env.example` lists every variable with placeholder values and comments
137	- [ ] No secrets appear in `.env.example`
138	- [ ] Client-safe variables prefixed with `NEXT_PUBLIC_` where needed (e.g., `NEXT_PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER`)
139	
140	**Technical notes:**
141	- Use `zod` for schema validation — parse `process.env` at module level
142	- Separate server-only and client-safe env into distinct exports to prevent accidental leakage
143	
144	**Dependencies:** 1.1
145	
146	**Estimate:** XS
147	
148	**Out of scope:** CI/CD environment configuration, deployment secrets management.
149	
150	---
151	
152	## Task 1.6: Set Up API Route Utilities and Error Handling
153	
154	**Description:** Create shared utilities for API route handlers: consistent response envelope, error handling wrapper, input validation helper, and rate limiting setup.
155	
156	**Acceptance criteria:**
157	- [ ] `lib/api/response.ts` exports `successResponse(data, status?)` and `errorResponse(message, status)` — both return `NextResponse` with consistent JSON shape `{ success, data, error }`
158	- [ ] `lib/api/handler.ts` exports a `withErrorHandling(handler)` wrapper that catches thrown errors, logs them server-side, and returns a sanitised `errorResponse`
159	- [ ] `lib/api/validate.ts` exports `validateBody(schema, body)` that parses with Zod and throws a structured 400 error on failure
160	- [ ] Prisma known errors mapped to user-friendly BM messages (P2002 -> "Rekod sudah wujud", P2025 -> "Rekod tidak dijumpai")
161	- [ ] Unknown errors return 500 with "Ralat dalaman pelayan" — no stack traces in response
162	- [ ] Rate limiting utility configured (in-memory for VPS, e.g., `lru-cache` based) — exportable per-route
163	
164	**Technical notes:**
165	- Response envelope: `{ success: boolean, data: T | null, error: string | null }`
166	- Rate limiter: simple sliding window with `lru-cache`, configurable per endpoint
167	- All user-facing error messages in Bahasa Malaysia
168	
169	**Dependencies:** 1.1, 1.2
170	
171	**Estimate:** S
172	
173	**Out of scope:** Specific endpoint implementations, admin-specific middleware.
174
```

```text
1	# Phase 2 — Design System & Primitives
2	
3	---
4	
5	## Task 2.1: Define Design Tokens in Tailwind Configuration
6	
7	**Description:** Extend the Tailwind config with the MawarBiru colour palette, typography scale, spacing, border-radius, and shadow tokens. These tokens are the single source of truth for the entire UI.
8	
9	**Acceptance criteria:**
10	- [ ] `tailwind.config.ts` extends `colors` with: `navy: '#07203F'`, `cream: '#EBDED4'`, `dusty-rose: '#D9AA90'`, `terracotta: '#A65E46'`, `dark: '#02000D'`
11	- [ ] Additional tints/shades generated for each colour (at minimum 3 variants per colour)
12	- [ ] Typography: `fontFamily.serif` set to a luxury serif font (e.g., Playfair Display) for headings, `fontFamily.sans` for body text
13	- [ ] Font size scale includes: `xs`, `sm`, `base`, `lg`, `xl`, `2xl`, `3xl`, `4xl`, `5xl` with matching line-heights
14	- [ ] Spacing tokens for consistent section padding: `section-y`, `section-x` with responsive values
15	- [ ] Border radius tokens: `rounded-card` (0.75rem), `rounded-button` (0.5rem), `rounded-badge` (9999px)
16	- [ ] Box shadow tokens: `shadow-card`, `shadow-elevated`, `shadow-dropdown`
17	- [ ] All colour combinations pass WCAG AA contrast ratio (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text)
18	- [ ] `globals.css` applies base styles: background `cream`, text `dark`, font `sans`
19	
20	**Technical notes:**
21	- Use Tailwind `theme.extend` to preserve defaults while adding custom tokens
22	- Import Google Fonts (Playfair Display + Inter) via `next/font`
23	- Verify contrast: `navy` on `cream` (AA pass), `cream` on `navy` (AA pass), `dark` on `cream` (AAA pass)
24	- Add CSS custom properties as fallback for dynamic colour needs
25	
26	**Dependencies:** 1.1
27	
28	**Estimate:** S
29	
30	**Out of scope:** Component implementation, responsive layout utilities, animations.
31	
32	---
33	
34	## Task 2.2: Build Core UI Primitive Components
35	
36	**Description:** Create the foundational UI primitives used across both client and admin: Button, Input, Textarea, Label, Badge, Card, Skeleton, Spinner. Each component is fully typed, accessible, and style-consistent with the design tokens.
37	
38	**Acceptance criteria:**
39	- [ ] `components/ui/button.tsx` — variants: `primary` (navy bg, cream text), `secondary` (cream bg, navy text/border), `accent` (dusty-rose bg), `ghost` (transparent), `danger` (red). Sizes: `sm`, `md`, `lg`. States: default, hover, focus (visible ring), active, disabled (opacity 50%), loading (spinner, aria-busy).
40	- [ ] `components/ui/input.tsx` — cream bg, navy border. States: default, hover, focus (ring-2 dusty-rose), error (border red + error message), disabled. Props: `label`, `error`, `helperText`, full `InputHTMLAttributes` pass-through.
41	- [ ] `components/ui/textarea.tsx` — same styling/states as Input, auto-resize optional.
42	- [ ] `components/ui/label.tsx` — associated with input via `htmlFor`, required indicator (`*` in terracotta).
43	- [ ] `components/ui/badge.tsx` — small pill, variants: `default`, `success`, `warning`, `error`, `custom` (accepts arbitrary bg colour for pakej calendar).
44	- [ ] `components/ui/card.tsx` — cream bg, `shadow-card`, `rounded-card`, optional hover lift. Compound: `Card`, `Card.Header`, `Card.Body`, `Card.Footer`.
45	- [ ] `components/ui/skeleton.tsx` — animated placeholder with `animate-pulse`. Variants: `text`, `circle`, `rect`.
46	- [ ] `components/ui/spinner.tsx` — SVG spinner, sizes `sm`/`md`/`lg`, `role="status"` + `aria-label="Memuatkan"`.
47	- [ ] All components use `forwardRef` for ref forwarding
48	- [ ] All components accept `className` for composition via `cn()` (clsx + tailwind-merge)
49	- [ ] All interactive components have visible focus indicators (WCAG 2.1)
50	- [ ] All components support `data-testid` prop
51	
52	**Technical notes:**
53	- Install `clsx` and `tailwind-merge`, create `lib/utils.ts` exporting `cn(...inputs)` utility
54	- Use `cva` (class-variance-authority) for variant management on Button and Badge
55	- Transitions: 150ms ease for hover/focus, 100ms for active — respect `prefers-reduced-motion`
56	
57	**Dependencies:** 2.1
58	
59	**Estimate:** L
60	
61	**Out of scope:** Form composition, specific page layouts, admin-only components.
62	
63	---
64	
65	## Task 2.3: Build Typography and Layout Components
66	
67	**Description:** Create reusable typography components (Heading, Text, SectionTitle) and layout primitives (Container, Section, Stack, Grid) that enforce the design system spacing and responsive behaviour.
68	
69	**Acceptance criteria:**
70	- [ ] `components/ui/heading.tsx` — renders `h1`-`h6` via `level` prop. Serif font. Sizes scale responsively. Colour defaults to `navy`.
71	- [ ] `components/ui/text.tsx` — renders `p` by default, `as` prop for `span`/`div`/`small`. Variants: `body`, `lead`, `small`, `muted`. Colour defaults to `dark`.
72	- [ ] `components/ui/section-title.tsx` — composed: Heading + optional subtitle Text + decorative underline (dusty-rose, 3rem wide, 2px thick, centred).
73	- [ ] `components/ui/container.tsx` — max-width 1200px, centred, responsive horizontal padding (px-4 mobile, px-6 tablet, px-8 desktop).
74	- [ ] `components/ui/section.tsx` — semantic `<section>` with vertical padding, optional `bg` variant (`cream`, `navy`, `white`). Includes Container internally.
75	- [ ] `components/ui/stack.tsx` — flex column/row with configurable `gap`. Props: `direction`, `gap`, `align`, `justify`.
76	- [ ] `components/ui/grid.tsx` — CSS Grid wrapper. Props: `cols` (responsive object `{ mobile: 1, tablet: 2, desktop: 3 }`), `gap`.
77	- [ ] Responsive breakpoints consistent: mobile (<640px), tablet (640-1023px), desktop (1024px+)
78	
79	**Technical notes:**
80	- Heading responsive sizing: `text-3xl md:text-4xl lg:text-5xl` pattern
81	- Container uses `mx-auto` + `w-full` + `max-w-[1200px]`
82	- Stack and Grid are thin convenience wrappers — avoid over-abstraction
83	
84	**Dependencies:** 2.1
85	
86	**Estimate:** M
87	
88	**Out of scope:** Page-specific layouts, navigation, footer.
89	
90	---
91	
92	## Task 2.4: Build Feedback and Overlay Components
93	
94	**Description:** Create components for user feedback and overlays: Toast/notification system, Modal/Dialog, Alert (inline), and EmptyState.
95	
96	**Acceptance criteria:**
97	- [ ] `components/ui/toast.tsx` + `lib/toast.ts` — toast notification system. Types: `success`, `error`, `info`, `warning`. Auto-dismiss after 5s. Stacks from bottom-right (desktop) / bottom-centre (mobile). Max 3 visible. `role="alert"` + `aria-live="polite"`. Imperative API: `toast.success(msg)`.
98	- [ ] `components/ui/modal.tsx` — accessible dialog. Focus trap on open, restores focus on close. Closes on Escape and backdrop click. Props: `isOpen`, `onClose`, `title`, `children`, `size`. Backdrop with navy overlay at 50% opacity. Entry/exit animation.
99	- [ ] `components/ui/alert.tsx` — inline alert banner. Variants: `info`, `success`, `warning`, `error`. Icon + message + optional action.
100	- [ ] `components/ui/empty-state.tsx` — illustration placeholder + heading + description + optional CTA button.
101	- [ ] All overlays respect `prefers-reduced-motion`
102	- [ ] All overlays are keyboard navigable
103	- [ ] Modal prevents body scroll when open
104	
105	**Technical notes:**
106	- Toast system: use `sonner` wrapped with custom styling to match design tokens, or React context + reducer
107	- Modal: `createPortal` to `document.body`, or use native `<dialog>` with `showModal()`
108	- Focus trap: use `focus-trap-react` or implement manually
109	
110	**Dependencies:** 2.1, 2.2
111	
112	**Estimate:** M
113	
114	**Out of scope:** Specific use-case modals, admin-specific dialogs.
115	
116	---
117	
118	## Task 2.5: Build Navigation Primitives and Floating WhatsApp Button
119	
120	**Description:** Create the shared navigation components (NavLink, MobileMenu) and the floating WhatsApp CTA button that appears on all client pages.
121	
122	**Acceptance criteria:**
123	- [ ] `components/ui/nav-link.tsx` — styled anchor/Link. States: default (cream text on navy bg), hover (dusty-rose underline), active/current page (terracotta underline, `aria-current="page"`), focus (visible ring). Uses `next/link` with `usePathname()`.
124	- [ ] `components/ui/mobile-menu.tsx` — slide-in drawer from right. Hamburger icon button (`aria-label="Buka menu"`, `aria-expanded`). Overlay: navy at 70% opacity. Menu: cream bg, full-height, nav links stacked. Close: X button + backdrop click + Escape. Focus trap while open. Body scroll lock. Transition: 200ms slide + fade.
125	- [ ] `components/client/whatsapp-fab.tsx` — floating action button, bottom-right, green WhatsApp icon (#25D366). Size: 56px circle. 17	- [ ] **States — slot hover:** slight lift, border darkens to terracotta
18	- [ ] **States — slot focus:** visible focus ring (dusty-rose)
19	- [ ] **States — slot active (pressed):** terracotta bg briefly, scale 0.98
20	- [ ] **States — slot selected:** navy bg, cream text, checkmark icon, `aria-pressed="true"`
21	- [ ] **Booking form:** appears below selected slot
22	- [ ] Form fields: `Nama` (required, text, min 2 chars, max 100), `No. Telefon` (required, tel, Malaysian format: 01X-XXXXXXX or 01X-XXXXXXXX), `Nota` (optional, textarea, max 500 chars)
23	- [ ] **States — form default:** submit disabled until Nama + No. Telefon filled and slot selected
24	- [ ] **States — form field error:** red border, error message below. Validates on blur + on submit. BM messages: "Sila masukkan nama anda", "Sila masukkan nombor telefon yang sah", etc.
25	- [ ] **States — form submitting:** submit button shows spinner + "Menghantar...", all fields disabled, `aria-busy="true"`
26	- [ ] **States — form success:** toast "Temujanji anda telah berjaya ditempah!", form resets, slot removed from available list. Inline success Alert with booking summary.
27	- [ ] **States — form error (server):** toast with error message. Form remains filled. Common: "Slot ini telah ditempah oleh orang lain" (409 race condition), "Ralat dalaman pelayan" (500).
28	- [ ] **States — empty (no slots):** EmptyState — "Tiada slot temujanji yang tersedia buat masa ini. Sila hubungi kami melalui WhatsApp." + WhatsApp link button.
29	- [ ] **Responsive — mobile:** full-width stacked, generous touch targets (min 44px)
30	- [ ] **Responsive — tablet:** slot buttons in 2-column grid, form full-width
31	- [ ] **Responsive — desktop:** slot buttons in 3-column grid per date, form in constrained-width card (~500px)
32	- [ ] **Accessibility:** slot buttons are `<button>` with `aria-label="Slot temujanji pada [tarikh] dari [masa mula] hingga [masa tamat]"`. Form fields have `<label>`. Errors linked via `aria-describedby`. Form has `aria-label="Borang tempahan temujanji"`. Success/error use `aria-live="polite"`.

36	- [ ] **Edge cases:** slot taken between load and submit (409 handling — refresh slot list). Double-submit prevented. Phone with/without dashes normalised.

53	
54	## Task 4.5: Build Temujanji API Routes
55	
56	**Description:** Implement the backend API routes that power the Temujanji page: fetching available slots and creating a new appointment booking.
57	
58	**Acceptance criteria:**
59	- [ ] `app/api/temujanji/slots/route.ts` — GET handler
60	  - Returns SlotTemujanji where `status = 'TERSEDIA'` and `tarikh >= today`
61	  - Grouped by `tarikh`, ordered by `tarikh` ASC then `masaMula` ASC
62	  - Limited to 14 days from today
63	  - Response envelope: `{ success: true, data: [...] }`
64	  - No authentication required (public endpoint)
65	  - Rate limited: 30 requests per minute per IP
66	- [ ] `app/api/temujanji/route.ts` — POST handler
67	  - Validates body with Zod: `{ nama: string (2-100), noTelefon: string (valid MY mobile), nota?: string (max 500), slotTemujanjiId: string }`
68	  - Returns 400 with field-level errors on validation failure
69	  - Finds or creates Pelanggan by `noTelefon` (upserts — updates `nama`/`nota` if existing)
70	  - Creates Temujanji record linked to Pelanggan and SlotTemujanji
71	  - Updates SlotTemujanji `status` from TERSEDIA to DITEMPAH
72	  - All in a single Prisma `$transaction` with `Serializable` isolation
73	  - Returns 409 if slot already DITEMPAH (race condition)
74	  - Returns 201 on success with booking summary
75	  - No authentication required
76	  - Rate limited: 5 requests per minute per IP
77	- [ ] Phone number normalisation: strips dashes before storage
78	- [ ] All error messages in Bahasa Malaysia
79	- [ ] Uses `withErrorHandling` and response helpers from 1.6
80	
81	**Technical notes:**
82	- Serializable transaction prevents double-booking race condition
83	- Phone upsert: `prisma.pelanggan.upsert({ where: { noTelefon }, create: {...}, update: { nama, nota } })`
84	- Zod schema exported from `lib/validations/temujanji.ts` for reuse in admin
85	- Date comparison: compare date-only, ignoring time component
86	
87	**Dependencies:** 1.2, 1.3, 1.6
88	
89	**Estimate:** M
90	
91	**Out of scope:** Slot creation (admin), appointment cancellation, notifications.
92

48	**Out of scope:** Booking from calendar, event creation (admin), week/day views, iCal export, Google Calendar sync.
49	
50	---
51	
52	## Task 4.6: Build Kalendar API Route
53	
54	**Description:** Implement the backend API route that powers the Kalendar page: fetching bookings for a given month with their associated package colours.
55	
56	**Acceptance criteria:**
57	- [ ] `app/api/kalendar/route.ts` — GET handler
58	  - Accepts query params: `bulan` (1-12), `tahun` (4-digit year)
59	  - Validates params with Zod — returns 400 if invalid
60	  - Returns all Tempahan for the month, including overflow days for calendar grid padding
61	  - Each Tempahan includes: `tarikh` (from SlotTempahan), `namaTempahan`, `pakej.namaPakej`, `pakej.warna`
62	  - Also returns distinct Pakej list (id, namaPakej, warna) for legend
63	  - Response: `{ success: true, data: { tempahan: [...], pakej: [...] } }`
64	  - Returns 200 with empty arrays if no bookings
65	  - No authentication required
66	  - Rate limited: 30 requests per minute per IP
67	  - Year range limited to current +/- 2 years
68	- [ ] Query joins SlotTempahan and Pakej on Tempahan
69	- [ ] Uses `withErrorHandling` wrapper from 1.6
70	- [ ] All error messages in Bahasa Malaysia
71	
72	**Technical notes:**
73	- Calculate visible date range: first day of month's week start (Sunday) to last day of month's week end (Saturday). Query SlotTempahan.tarikh between these bounds.
74	- Prisma query: join Tempahan -> SlotTempahan + Pakej, select relevant fields
75	- Pakej list for legend: `prisma.pakej.findMany({ select: { id, namaPakej, warna } })`
76	- Caching: `Cache-Control: public, max-age=60` since bookings don't change frequently
77	
78	**Dependencies:** 1.2, 1.3, 1.6
79	
80	**Estimate:** S
81	
82	**Out of scope:** Booking creation, admin calendar, recurring events, export.
83
```

```text
1	# MawarBiru — Spec
2	
3	## TL;DR
4	
5	MawarBiru is a wedding and event venue website for a small business owner. Public visitors (primarily from TikTok) can browse the venue's offerings, book consultation appointments (temujanji), and check venue availability via an event calendar. The business owner manages everything — slots, bookings, packages, and customers — through a protected admin panel. Built as a Next.js monolith with MySQL, styled in a luxury aesthetic with a navy-and-cream palette.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. Problem & Users
10	
11	**Problem:** The business owner promotes his venue on TikTok but has no website for interested visitors to learn more, check availability, or book consultations. All coordination happens manually via WhatsApp.
12	
13	**Users:**
14	
15	| User | Description |
16	|------|-------------|
17	| **Pelawat (Visitor)** | Arrives from TikTok. Browses packages, checks venue availability, books a consultation. No account required. |
18	| **Admin (Business Owner)** | Single user. Manages all content, slots, bookings, and customer data via admin panel. |
19	
20	**One-sentence pitch:** A luxury venue showcase site with consultation booking and an admin CMS for the business owner.
21	
22	---
23	
24	## 2. Core User Journeys
25	
26	### Journey 1: Visitor Books a Consultation (Temujanji)
27	
28	1. Visitor sees TikTok video with link to `mawarbiru.my/temujanji`
29	2. Visitor lands on `/temujanji` page
30	3. Visitor sees available consultation slots (created by admin)
31	4. Visitor selects a slot
32	5. Visitor fills in: **Nama**, **No. Telefon**, **Nota**
33	6. Visitor submits — booking confirmed
34	7. A **Pelanggan** record is automatically created from the form data
35	
36	### Journey 2: Visitor Checks Venue Availability
37	
38	1. Visitor navigates to `/kalendar`
39	2. Visitor sees a monthly calendar view showing all booked events (colour-coded by category)
40	3. Visitor can navigate between months
41	4. **Read-only** — no actions available, just viewing
42	
43	### Journey 3: Admin Creates a Tempahan (Booking)
44	
45	1. Admin logs in to admin panel
46	2. Navigates to **Pengurusan Tempahan**
47	3. Views calendar of available slots (Slot Tempahan)
48	4. Clicks on an available date — redirected to tempahan creation form
49	5. Fills in: **Nama Tempahan**, selects **Pakej**, selects **Pelanggan**
50	6. Submits — tempahan created, slot marked as booked
51	7. Event appears on public `/kalendar`
52	
53	---
54	
55	## 3. Scope — MVP vs Later
56	
57	### In (MVP)
58	
59	- Single admin login (email + password)
60	- Admin modules: Slot Tempahan, Slot Temujanji, Tempahan, Temujanji, Pakej, Pelanggan
61	- Public pages: Homepage (`/`), Temujanji (`/temujanji`), Kalendar (`/kalendar`), Pakej (`/pakej` — static)
62	- Floating WhatsApp button on all public pages
63	- Mobile-first responsive design for customer-facing pages
64	- Luxury design with brand colour palette
65	- Bahasa Malaysia only
66	
67	### Out (Later)
68	
69	- Customer accounts / login
70	- Online payment
71	- Email / SMS notifications
72	- Analytics integration
73	- Multi-user admin / staff roles
74	- English language toggle
75	- List view for kalendar
76	- Dynamic pakej page (pulled from admin data)
77	
78	---
79	
80	## 4. Functional Requirements
81	
82	### 4.1 Public Pages
83	
84	#### Homepage (`/`)
85	
86	- Hero section with venue branding ("MawarBiru" text logo)
87	- Introduction to the business
88	- CTA buttons to `/temujanji` and `/kalendar`
89	- Floating WhatsApp button
90	
91	#### Temujanji (`/temujanji`)
92	
93	- Displays available consultation slots (from admin-managed Slot Temujanji)
94	- Visitor selects a slot and fills in:
95	  - Nama (required)
96	  - No. Telefon (required)
97	  - Nota (optional)
98	- On submit: creates Temujanji record + auto-creates Pelanggan record
99	- Booked slots no longer appear as available
100	
101	#### Kalendar (`/kalendar`)
102	
103	- Monthly calendar view
104	- Shows all Tempahan entries with:
105	  - Event name
106	  - Category (colour-coded: kahwin, aqiqah, open house, etc.)
107	  - Date
108	- Month navigation (previous/next)
109	- Read-only — no user interaction beyond navigation
110	
111	#### Pakej (`/pakej`)
112	
113	- Static page — content is hardcoded, not pulled from admin
114	- Showcases wedding and event packages
115	- Designed to match the luxury aesthetic
116	
117	### 4.2 Admin Modules
118	
119	All admin routes are protected behind authentication.
120	
121	#### Pengurusan Slot Temujanji
122	
123	- CRUD for consultation time slots
124	- Fields: Tarikh, Masa, Status (Tersedia / Ditempah)
125	- Admin can create, edit, delete slots
126	
127	#### Pengurusan Temujanji
128	
129	- View all consultation bookings
130	- Each record shows: Pelanggan info, Slot details, Nota
131	- Admin can update status or delete
132	
133	#### Pengurusan Slot Tempahan
134	
135	- CRUD for venue booking date slots
136	- Fields: Tarikh, Status (Tersedia / Ditempah)
137	- Calendar view for managing slots
138	
139	#### Pengurusan Tempahan
140	
141	- Create booking from calendar: click available slot → redirected to creation form
142	- Creation form fields:
143	  - Nama Tempahan (required)
144	  - Pakej (select from existing packages)
145	  - Pelanggan (select from existing customers)
146	  - Kategori: Kahwin, Aqiqah, Open House, etc.
147	- View, edit, delete existing bookings
148	- Booked events appear on public `/kalendar`
149	
150	#### Pengurusan Pakej
151	
152	- CRUD for packages
153	- Fields:
154	  - Nama Pakej (required)
155	  - Kategori (Perkahwinan, Acara, etc.)
156	  - Gambar (image upload)
157	  - Tiers — dynamic list of: Bilangan Pax + Harga
158	  - Sections — dynamic list of: Tajuk Section + bullet list of items
159	- Pakej data is used when creating Tempahan (admin-only, not public-facing)
160	
161	#### Pengurusan Pelanggan
162	
163	- View all customers
164	- Created automatically when visitor books temujanji, or manually by admin
165	- Fields: Nama, No. Telefon, Nota
166	- Edit and delete
167	
168	---
169	
170	## 5. Data Model
171	
172	### Entities
173	
174	| Entity | Fields | Notes |
175	|--------|--------|-------|
176	| **User** | id, email, password, createdAt, updatedAt | Single admin user |
177	| **Pelanggan** | id, nama, noTelefon, nota, createdAt, updatedAt | Auto-created from temujanji or manually |
178	| **SlotTemujanji** | id, tarikh, masa, status, createdAt, updatedAt | Status: TERSEDIA, DITEMPAH |
179	| **Temujanji** | id, pelangganId, slotTemujanjiId, nota, createdAt, updatedAt | Links to Pelanggan and SlotTemujanji |
180	| **SlotTempahan** | id, tarikh, status, createdAt, updatedAt | Status: TERSEDIA, DITEMPAH |
181	| **Tempahan** | id, namaTempahan, slotTempahanId, pakejId, pelangganId, kategori, createdAt, updatedAt | Kategori: KAHWIN, AQIQAH, OPEN_HOUSE, etc. |
182	| **Pakej** | id, namaPakej, kategori, gambar, createdAt, updatedAt | Parent entity for tiers and sections |
183	| **PakejTier** | id, pakejId, bilanganPax, harga | e.g., 300 PAX → RM15,500 |
184	| **PakejSection** | id, pakejId, tajuk, items (JSON or related table) | e.g., "Menu Kenduri" → list of food items |
185	
186	### Relationships
187	
188	```
189	User (standalone — single admin)
190	
191	Pelanggan 1 ← N Temujanji
192	SlotTemujanji 1 ← 1 Temujanji
193	
194	Pelanggan 1 ← N Tempahan
195	SlotTempahan 1 ← 1 Tempahan
196	Pakej 1 ← N Tempahan
197	
198	Pakej 1 ← N PakejTier
199	Pakej 1 ← N PakejSection
200	```
201	
202	### Data Privacy
203	
204	- No sensitive data requiring encryption beyond admin password (hashed)
205	- Customer data (nama, phone) is basic contact info
206	- No payment data stored
207	
208	---
209	
210	## 6. Auth & Permissions
211	
212	| Aspect | Decision |
213	|--------|----------|
214	| Auth method | Email + password |
215	| Users | Single admin account |
216	| Customer login | None — visitors are anonymous |
217	| Session | Server-side session or JWT |
218	| Roles | No role system — single admin only |
219	| Multi-tenant | No |
220	
221	---
222	
223	## 7. Tech Stack
224	
225	| Layer | Choice | Rationale |
226	|-------|--------|-----------|
227	| Framework | **Next.js** (monolith) | Full-stack, SSR for public pages, API routes for admin |
228	| Language | **TypeScript** | Type safety |
229	| Database | **MySQL** | Familiar, reliable |
230	| ORM | **Prisma** | Best DX with Next.js, type-safe queries |
231	| Styling | **Tailwind CSS** | Rapid UI development, mobile-first utilities |
232	| Hosting | **VPS** | Full control, cost-effective |
233	| Auth | **NextAuth.js** (or similar) | Simple credentials provider for single admin |
234	
235	---
236	
237	## 8. Integrations
238	
239	| Integration | Status |
240	|-------------|--------|
241	| WhatsApp | Floating button linking to `wa.me/<number>` |
242	| Payment gateway | Not in MVP |
243	| Email / SMS | Not in MVP |
244	| Analytics | Not in MVP |
245	
246	---
247	
248	## 9. Non-Functional Requirements
249	
250	| Aspect | Requirement |
251	|--------|-------------|
252	| Responsive | Mobile-first for all customer-facing pages |
253	| Language | Bahasa Malaysia only |
254	| Browser support | Modern browsers (Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge) |
255	| Performance | Fast initial load — critical for TikTok traffic with short attention spans |
256	| Accessibility | Basic — semantic HTML, readable contrast |
257	| SEO | Basic meta tags, SSR for public pages |
258	
259	---
260	
261	## 10. Design
262	
263	### Branding
264	
265	- **Logo:** Text-based "MawarBiru" (no graphic logo yet)
266	- **Style:** Luxury / mewah
267	
268	### Colour Palette
269	
270	| Role | Hex | Description | Usage |
271	|------|-----|-------------|-------|
272	| Primary | `#07203F` | Deep navy | Headers, nav, buttons, key UI elements |
273	| Secondary | `#EBDED4` | Warm cream | Page backgrounds, cards |
274	| Accent 1 | `#D9AA90` | Dusty rose / peach | CTAs, highlights, hover states |
275	| Accent 2 | `#A65E46` | Terracotta | Active states, badges, small accents |
276	| Dark | `#02000D` | Near-black | Body text, footer |
277	
278	### Design Decisions
279	
280	- Navy + cream base for luxury feel with high contrast
281	- Dusty rose for CTAs — draws attention without being loud
282	- Terracotta used sparingly for warmth
283	- Near-black for text instead of pure black — softer on eyes
284

# MawarBiru — Phase 1 Foundation Implementation

## Context
Project has task specs ready in `claude/resources/tasks/`. No code exists yet. Next.js scaffolding partially done. Need to complete Phase 1 (foundation) tasks to unblock all subsequent phases.

## Plan
1. **Task 1.1** — Complete Next.js scaffold (folder structure, tsconfig strict, prettier, .gitignore) — partially done
2. **Task 1.2** — Prisma schema with all 9 models (parallel with 1.5)
3. **Task 1.5** — Environment validation with Zod (parallel with 1.2)
4. **Task 1.3** — Prisma client singleton + seed script (after 1.2)
5. **Task 1.6** — API route utilities (after 1.2)
6. **Task 1.4** — NextAuth.js admin auth (after 1.3)

Tasks 1.2 and 1.5 run in parallel via subagents. Then 1.3+1.6 in parallel. Then 1.4.

## Files
- `tsconfig.json`, `tailwind.config.ts`, `.gitignore`, `package.json`
- `prisma/schema.prisma`, `lib/db.ts`, `prisma/seed.ts`
- `lib/env.ts`, `.env.example`
- `lib/api/response.ts`, `lib/api/handler.ts`, `lib/api/validate.ts`
- `app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts`, `lib/auth.ts`, `middleware.ts`

## Verification
- `npm run dev` starts without errors
- `npx prisma validate` passes
- `npx prisma db seed` runs clean
- Auth middleware protects `/admin/*` routes

Everything is set up. Here is a summary of what was done:

**Created/modified files:**

- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/prisma/schema.prisma` -- Complete schema with all 9 models (`User`, `Pelanggan`, `SlotTemujanji`, `Temujanji`, `SlotTempahan`, `Tempahan`, `Pakej`, `PakejTier`, `PakejSection`), the `StatusSlot` enum, and all relations with appropriate `onDelete` behavior (Cascade for parent-child, Restrict for Tempahan->Pelanggan).

- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/prisma.config.ts` -- Required by Prisma v7 (replaces the `url` property in the datasource block). Points to `DATABASE_URL` env var for migrations.

- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/.env` -- Created with `DATABASE_URL` for local dev.

- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/.env.example` -- Already existed with `DATABASE_URL` and other env vars; left unchanged.

**Notable adaptation:** Prisma v7.7.0 no longer supports `url` in the `datasource` block of `schema.prisma`. The connection URL must now be configured via `prisma.config.ts` for migrations, and passed to the `PrismaClient` constructor or via adapter for runtime. The schema and config were written to comply with this new requirement.

**Validation and generation both passed successfully.**

You are working on the MawarBiru project at /home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru — a Next.js wedding venue website.

**Task: Install Prisma and define the complete MySQL schema.**

1. Install prisma and @prisma/client:
   ```
   cd /home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru && npm install prisma @prisma/client
   ```

2. Initialize prisma with mysql:
   ```
   npx prisma init --datasource-provider mysql
   ```

3. Create the schema in `prisma/schema.prisma` with these 9 models:

**Enums:**
- `StatusSlot`: TERSEDIA, DITEMPAH, DIBATALKAN

**Models:**

- `User`: id (String @id @default(cuid())), email (String @unique), passwordHash (String), nama (String), createdAt (DateTime @default(now()))

- `Pelanggan`: id (String @id @default(cuid())), nama (String), noTelefon (String @unique), nota (String? @db.Text), createdAt (DateTime @default(now()))

- `SlotTemujanji`: id (String @id @default(cuid())), tarikh (DateTime @db.Date), masaMula (String), masaTamat (String), status (StatusSlot @default(TERSEDIA)), createdAt (DateTime @default(now())), temujanji (Temujanji?)

- `Temujanji`: id (String @id @default(cuid())), pelangganId (String), slotTemujanjiId (String @unique), nota (String? @db.Text), createdAt (DateTime @default(now())), pelanggan (Pelanggan @relation), slotTemujanji (SlotTemujanji @relation)

- `SlotTempahan`: id (String @id @default(cuid())), tarikh (DateTime @db.Date), status (StatusSlot @default(TERSEDIA)), createdAt (DateTime @default(now())), tempahan (Tempahan?)

- `Tempahan`: id (String @id @default(cuid())), namaTempahan (String), slotTempahanId (String @unique), pakejId (String), pelangganId (String), nota (String? @db.Text), createdAt (DateTime @default(now())), slotTempahan (SlotTempahan @relation), pakej (Pakej @relation), pelanggan (Pelanggan @relation)

- `Pakej`: id (String @id @default(cuid())), namaPakej (String @unique), warna (String), gambar (String?), createdAt (DateTime @default(now())), tiers (PakejTier[]), sections (PakejSection[]), tempahan (Tempahan[])

- `PakejTier`: id (String @id @default(cuid())), pakejId (String), namaTier (String), bilanganPax (Int), harga (Decimal @db.Decimal(10, 2)), createdAt (DateTime @default(now())), pakej (Pakej @relation)

- `PakejSection`: id (String @id @default(cuid())), pakejId (String), tajuk (String), items (Json), createdAt (DateTime @default(now())), pakej (Pakej @relation)

Relations:
- Pelanggan has many Temujanji and Tempahan
- SlotTemujanji has one optional Temujanji (unique)
- SlotTempahan has one optional Tempahan (unique)
- Pakej has many PakejTier, PakejSection, Tempahan
- All relations use onDelete: Cascade where child depends on parent, Restrict for Tempahan->Pelanggan

4. Run `npx prisma validate` to verify the schema is correct.

5. Create `.env.example` at project root with:
```
DATABASE_URL="mysql://user:password@localhost:3306/mawarbiru"
```

6. Make sure the `.env` file (not .env.example) also has the DATABASE_URL for local dev.

7. Run `npx prisma generate` to generate the client.

You are working on the MawarBiru project at /home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru — a Next.js wedding venue website.

**Task: Install Zod and create environment variable validation.**

1. Install zod:
   ```
   cd /home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru && npm install zod
   ```

2. Create `lib/env.ts` that:
   - Uses Zod to validate `process.env` at import time
   - Required server vars: DATABASE_URL, NEXTAUTH_SECRET, NEXTAUTH_URL, ADMIN_EMAIL
   - Optional vars: WHATSAPP_NUMBER (defaults to empty string)
   - Client-safe vars: NEXT_PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER (optional, defaults to empty)
   - Export separate `serverEnv` and `clientEnv` objects
   - If validation fails, throw with clear error message listing missing vars
   - Server env must NOT be importable from client components

Example structure:
```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';

const serverSchema = z.object({
  DATABASE_URL: z.string().min(1, 'DATABASE_URL is required'),
  NEXTAUTH_SECRET: z.string().min(1, 'NEXTAUTH_SECRET is required'),
  NEXTAUTH_URL: z.string().url('NEXTAUTH_URL must be a valid URL'),
  ADMIN_EMAIL: z.string().email('ADMIN_EMAIL must be a valid email'),
  WHATSAPP_NUMBER: z.string().default(''),
});

const clientSchema = z.object({
  NEXT_PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER: z.string().default(''),
});

// Parse and export
```

3. Update the `.env.example` file at project root to include ALL variables with placeholder values and comments:
```
# Database
DATABASE_URL="mysql://user:password@localhost:3306/mawarbiru"

# NextAuth
NEXTAUTH_SECRET="your-secret-key-here"
NEXTAUTH_URL="http://localhost:3000"

# Admin
ADMIN_EMAIL="admin@mawarbiru.my"

# WhatsApp (optional)
WHATSAPP_NUMBER=""
NEXT_PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER=""
```

import { z } from 'zod';

// ── Server-side environment variables ──────────────────────────────────
const serverSchema = z.object({
  DATABASE_URL: z.string().min(1, 'DATABASE_URL is required'),
  NEXTAUTH_SECRET: z.string().min(1, 'NEXTAUTH_SECRET is required'),
  NEXTAUTH_URL: z.string().url('NEXTAUTH_URL must be a valid URL'),
  ADMIN_EMAIL: z.string().email('ADMIN_EMAIL must be a valid email'),
  WHATSAPP_NUMBER: z.string().default(''),
});

// ── Client-safe environment variables (NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix) ────────────
const clientSchema = z.object({
  NEXT_PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER: z.string().default(''),
});

// ── Guard: prevent server env from leaking to client bundles ───────────
function validateServerEnv() {
  if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
    throw new Error(
      'Server environment variables must not be imported in client components. ' +
        'Use `clientEnv` instead.',
    );
  }

  const result = serverSchema.safeParse(process.env);

  if (!result.success) {
    const missing = result.error.issues
      .map((issue) => `  - ${issue.path.join('.')}: ${issue.message}`)
      .join('\n');

    throw new Error(
      `Missing or invalid server environment variables:\n${missing}`,
    );
  }

  return result.data;
}

function validateClientEnv() {
  const result = clientSchema.safeParse({
    NEXT_PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER:
      process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER ?? '',
  });

  if (!result.success) {
    const missing = result.error.issues
      .map((issue) => `  - ${issue.path.join('.')}: ${issue.message}`)
      .join('\n');

    throw new Error(
      `Missing or invalid client environment variables:\n${missing}`,
    );
  }

  return result.data;
}

// ── Exported validated env objects ──────────────────────────────────────

/**
 * Server-only environment variables.
 * Throws at import time if any required variable is missing or invalid.
 * Throws if imported from a client component (browser context).
 */
export const serverEnv = validateServerEnv();

/**
 * Client-safe environment variables (NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix only).
 * Safe to import from both server and client components.
 */
export const clientEnv = validateClientEnv();

```

```prisma
generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "mysql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

enum StatusSlot {
  TERSEDIA
  DITEMPAH
  DIBATALKAN
}

model User {
  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
  email        String   @unique
  passwordHash String
  nama         String
  createdAt    DateTime @default(now())

  @@map("users")
}

model Pelanggan {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  nama      String
  noTelefon String   @unique
  nota      String?  @db.Text
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  temujanji Temujanji[]
  tempahan  Tempahan[]

  @@map("pelanggan")
}

model SlotTemujanji {
  id        String     @id @default(cuid())
  tarikh    DateTime   @db.Date
  masaMula  String
  masaTamat String
  status    StatusSlot @default(TERSEDIA)
  createdAt DateTime   @default(now())

  temujanji Temujanji?

  @@map("slot_temujanji")
}

model Temujanji {
  id              String   @id @default(cuid())
  pelangganId     String
  slotTemujanjiId String   @unique
  nota            String?  @db.Text
  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())

  pelanggan     Pelanggan     @relation(fields: [pelangganId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  slotTemujanji SlotTemujanji @relation(fields: [slotTemujanjiId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@map("temujanji")
}

model SlotTempahan {
  id        String     @id @default(cuid())
  tarikh    DateTime   @db.Date
  status    StatusSlot @default(TERSEDIA)
  createdAt DateTime   @default(now())

  tempahan Tempahan?

  @@map("slot_tempahan")
}

model Tempahan {
  id             String   @id @default(cuid())
  namaTempahan   String
  slotTempahanId String   @unique
  pakejId        String
  pelangganId    String
  nota           String?  @db.Text
  createdAt      DateTime @default(now())

  slotTempahan SlotTempahan @relation(fields: [slotTempahanId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  pakej        Pakej        @relation(fields: [pakejId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  pelanggan    Pelanggan    @relation(fields: [pelangganId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)

  @@map("tempahan")
}

model Pakej {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  namaPakej String   @unique
  warna     String
  gambar    String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  tiers    PakejTier[]
  sections PakejSection[]
  tempahan Tempahan[]

  @@map("pakej")
}

model PakejTier {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  pakejId     String
  namaTier    String
  bilanganPax Int
  harga       Decimal  @db.Decimal(10, 2)
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())

  pakej Pakej @relation(fields: [pakejId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@map("pakej_tier")
}

model PakejSection {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  pakejId   String
  tajuk     String
  items     Json
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  pakej Pakej @relation(fields: [pakejId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@map("pakej_section")
}

```

You are working on the MawarBiru project at /home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru — a Next.js 16 wedding venue website.

**Task: Create shared API route utilities.**

1. **Install lru-cache**: `npm install lru-cache`

2. **Create `lib/api/response.ts`**:
```typescript
import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';

type ApiResponse<T> = {
  success: boolean;
  data: T | null;
  error: string | null;
};

export function successResponse<T>(data: T, status = 200) {
  return NextResponse.json<ApiResponse<T>>(
    { success: true, data, error: null },
    { status }
  );
}

export function errorResponse(message: string, status = 500) {
  return NextResponse.json<ApiResponse<null>>(
    { success: false, data: null, error: message },
    { status }
  );
}
```

3. **Create `lib/api/handler.ts`**:
```typescript
import { NextRequest } from 'next/server';
import { errorResponse } from './response';
import { Prisma } from '@prisma/client';

// Map Prisma error codes to BM messages
const prismaErrorMessages: Record<string, { message: string; status: number }> = {
  P2002: { message: 'Rekod sudah wujud', status: 409 },
  P2025: { message: 'Rekod tidak dijumpai', status: 404 },
};

type RouteHandler = (req: NextRequest, context?: any) => Promise<Response>;

export function withErrorHandling(handler: RouteHandler): RouteHandler {
  return async (req, context) => {
    try {
      return await handler(req, context);
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('[API Error]', error);
      
      if (error instanceof Prisma.PrismaClientKnownRequestError) {
        const mapped = prismaErrorMessages[error.code];
        if (mapped) {
          return errorResponse(mapped.message, mapped.status);
        }
      }
      
      if (error instanceof ZodValidationError) {
        return errorResponse(error.message, 400);
      }
      
      return errorResponse('Ralat dalaman pelayan', 500);
    }
  };
}
```
Note: ZodValidationError should be a custom error class you define — or use the zod error directly. Adjust the implementation to handle zod validation errors thrown by validateBody.

4. **Create `lib/api/validate.ts`**:
```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';

export class ValidationError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = 'ValidationError';
  }
}

export function validateBody<T>(schema: z.ZodSchema<T>, body: unknown): T {
  const result = schema.safeParse(body);
  if (!result.success) {
    const firstError = result.error.errors[0];
    throw new ValidationError(firstError?.message ?? 'Data tidak sah');
  }
  return result.data;
}
```

5. **Create `lib/api/rate-limit.ts`**:
```typescript
import { LRUCache } from 'lru-cache';
import { NextRequest } from 'next/server';
import { errorResponse } from './response';

type RateLimitConfig = {
  interval: number; // ms
  maxRequests: number;
};

export function createRateLimiter(config: RateLimitConfig) {
  const cache = new LRUCache<string, number[]>({
    max: 500,
    ttl: config.interval,
  });

  return function rateLimit(req: NextRequest): Response | null {
    const ip = req.headers.get('x-forwarded-for') ?? req.headers.get('x-real-ip') ?? 'unknown';
    const now = Date.now();
    const windowStart = now - config.interval;
    
    const timestamps = cache.get(ip) ?? [];
    const recentTimestamps = timestamps.filter(t => t > windowStart);
    
    if (recentTimestamps.length >= config.maxRequests) {
      return errorResponse('Terlalu banyak permintaan. Sila cuba sebentar lagi.', 429) as any;
    }
    
    recentTimestamps.push(now);
    cache.set(ip, recentTimestamps);
    return null; // not rate limited
  };
}
```

import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';

type ApiResponse<T> = {
  readonly success: boolean;
  readonly data: T | null;
  readonly error: string | null;
};

export function successResponse<T>(data: T, status = 200) {
  return NextResponse.json<ApiResponse<T>>(
    { success: true, data, error: null },
    { status },
  );
}

export function errorResponse(message: string, status = 500) {
  return NextResponse.json<ApiResponse<null>>(
    { success: false, data: null, error: message },
    { status },
  );
}

```

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';

export class ValidationError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = 'ValidationError';
  }
}

export function validateBody<T>(schema: z.ZodSchema<T>, body: unknown): T {
  const result = schema.safeParse(body);
  if (!result.success) {
    const firstError = result.error.errors[0];
    throw new ValidationError(firstError?.message ?? 'Data tidak sah');
  }
  return result.data;
}

```

```typescript
import { LRUCache } from 'lru-cache';
import { NextRequest } from 'next/server';
import { errorResponse } from './response';

type RateLimitConfig = {
  readonly interval: number; // ms
  readonly maxRequests: number;
};

export function createRateLimiter(config: RateLimitConfig) {
  const cache = new LRUCache<string, readonly number[]>({
    max: 500,
    ttl: config.interval,
  });

  return function rateLimit(req: NextRequest): Response | null {
    const ip =
      req.headers.get('x-forwarded-for') ??
      req.headers.get('x-real-ip') ??
      'unknown';
    const now = Date.now();
    const windowStart = now - config.interval;

    const timestamps = cache.get(ip) ?? [];
    const recentTimestamps = timestamps.filter((t) => t > windowStart);

    if (recentTimestamps.length >= config.maxRequests) {
      return errorResponse(
        'Terlalu banyak permintaan. Sila cuba sebentar lagi.',
        429,
      );
    }

    cache.set(ip, [...recentTimestamps, now]);
    return null; // not rate limited
  };
}

```

```typescript
import { NextRequest } from 'next/server';
import { errorResponse } from './response';
import { ValidationError } from './validate';
import { Prisma } from '@prisma/client';

// Map Prisma error codes to Bahasa Malaysia messages
const prismaErrorMessages: Readonly<
  Record<string, { readonly message: string; readonly status: number }>
> = {
  P2002: { message: 'Rekod sudah wujud', status: 409 },
  P2025: { message: 'Rekod tidak dijumpai', status: 404 },
};

type RouteHandler = (req: NextRequest, context?: unknown) => Promise<Response>;

export function withErrorHandling(handler: RouteHandler): RouteHandler {
  return async (req, context) => {
    try {
      return await handler(req, context);
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('[API Error]', error);

      if (error instanceof Prisma.PrismaClientKnownRequestError) {
        const mapped = prismaErrorMessages[error.code];
        if (mapped) {
          return errorResponse(mapped.message, mapped.status);
        }
      }

      if (error instanceof ValidationError) {
        return errorResponse(error.message, 400);
      }

      return errorResponse('Ralat dalaman pelayan', 500);
    }
  };
}

```

import { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';

const prisma = new PrismaClient();

interface TierData {
  readonly namaTier: string;
  readonly bilanganPax: number;
  readonly harga: number;
}

interface SectionData {
  readonly tajuk: string;
  readonly items: readonly string[];
}

interface PakejData {
  readonly namaPakej: string;
  readonly warna: string;
  readonly tiers: readonly TierData[];
  readonly sections: readonly SectionData[];
}

async function seedAdmin(): Promise<void> {
  const email = process.env.ADMIN_EMAIL;
  const passwordHash = process.env.ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH;

  if (!email || !passwordHash) {
    console.log(
      'Skipping admin seed: ADMIN_EMAIL or ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH not set.',
    );
    return;
  }

  await prisma.user.upsert({
    where: { email },
    update: { passwordHash, nama: 'Admin' },
    create: { email, passwordHash, nama: 'Admin' },
  });

  console.log(`Admin user upserted: ${email}`);
}

async function seedPakej(data: PakejData): Promise<void> {
  const pakej = await prisma.pakej.upsert({
    where: { namaPakej: data.namaPakej },
    update: { warna: data.warna },
    create: { namaPakej: data.namaPakej, warna: data.warna },
  });

  // Delete existing tiers and sections, then recreate
  await prisma.pakejTier.deleteMany({ where: { pakejId: pakej.id } });
  await prisma.pakejSection.deleteMany({ where: { pakejId: pakej.id } });

  await prisma.pakejTier.createMany({
    data: data.tiers.map((tier) => ({
      pakejId: pakej.id,
      namaTier: tier.namaTier,
      bilanganPax: tier.bilanganPax,
      harga: tier.harga,
    })),
  });

  await prisma.pakejSection.createMany({
    data: data.sections.map((section) => ({
      pakejId: pakej.id,
      tajuk: section.tajuk,
      items: section.items as unknown as string[],
    })),
  });

  console.log(`Pakej seeded: ${data.namaPakej}`);
}

        'Air Sirap Sejuk',
      ],
    },
    {
      tajuk: 'Hall Rental',
      items: [
        'Berhawa Dingin',
        'Pentas Berdeko',
        'PA Sistem Tanpa Emcee',
        'Dome VIP (1)',
        'Pelayan Am',
      ],
    },
  ],
};

async function main(): Promise<void> {
  console.log('Seeding database...');

  await seedAdmin();
  await seedPakej(nightWedding);
  await seedPakej(pakejWedding);
  await seedPakej(eventPackage);

  console.log('Seeding complete.');
}

main()
  .catch((e: unknown) => {
    console.error('Seed failed:', e);
    process.exit(1);
  })
  .finally(async () => {
    await prisma.$disconnect();
  });

```

5. **Update `.env.example`** to include `ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH` variable if not already there.

46	  temujanji Temujanji?
47	
48	  @@map("slot_temujanji")
49	}
50	
51	model Temujanji {
52	  id              String   @id @default(cuid())
53	  pelangganId     String
54	  slotTemujanjiId String   @unique
55	  nota            String?  @db.Text
56	  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())
57	
58	  pelanggan     Pelanggan     @relation(fields: [pelangganId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
59	  slotTemujanji SlotTemujanji @relation(fields: [slotTemujanjiId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
60	
61	  @@map("temujanji")
62	}
63	
64	model SlotTempahan {
65	  id        String     @id @default(cuid())

69	
70	  tempahan Tempahan?
71	
72	  @@map("slot_tempahan")
73	}

75	model Tempahan {

77	  namaTempahan   String
78	  slotTempahanId String   @unique
79	  pakejId        String
80	  pelangganId    String

84	  slotTempahan SlotTempahan @relation(fields: [slotTempahanId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
85	  pakej        Pakej        @relation(fields: [pakejId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
86	  pelanggan    Pelanggan    @relation(fields: [pelangganId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
87	
88	  @@map("tempahan")
89	}
90	
91	model Pakej {
92	  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
93	  namaPakej String   @unique

97	
98	  tiers    PakejTier[]

101	
102	  @@map("pakej")

107	  pakejId     String
108	  namaTier    String
109	  bilanganPax Int

114	
115	  @@map("pakej_tier")

126

1	import { z } from 'zod';
2	
3	// ── Server-side environment variables ──────────────────────────────────
4	const serverSchema = z.object({
5	  DATABASE_URL: z.string().min(1, 'DATABASE_URL is required'),
6	  NEXTAUTH_SECRET: z.string().min(1, 'NEXTAUTH_SECRET is required'),
7	  NEXTAUTH_URL: z.string().url('NEXTAUTH_URL must be a valid URL'),
8	  ADMIN_EMAIL: z.string().email('ADMIN_EMAIL must be a valid email'),
9	  WHATSAPP_NUMBER: z.string().default(''),
10	});
11	
12	// ── Client-safe environment variables (NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix) ────────────
13	const clientSchema = z.object({
14	  NEXT_PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER: z.string().default(''),
15	});
16	
17	// ── Guard: prevent server env from leaking to client bundles ───────────
18	function validateServerEnv() {
19	  if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
20	    throw new Error(
21	      'Server environment variables must not be imported in client components. ' +
22	        'Use `clientEnv` instead.',
23	    );
24	  }
25	
26	  const result = serverSchema.safeParse(process.env);
27	
28	  if (!result.success) {
29	    const missing = result.error.issues
30	      .map((issue) => `  - ${issue.path.join('.')}: ${issue.message}`)
31	      .join('\n');
32	
33	    throw new Error(
34	      `Missing or invalid server environment variables:\n${missing}`,
35	    );
36	  }
37	
38	  return result.data;
39	}
40	
41	function validateClientEnv() {
42	  const result = clientSchema.safeParse({
43	    NEXT_PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER:
44	      process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER ?? '',
45	  });
46	
47	  if (!result.success) {
48	    const missing = result.error.issues
49	      .map((issue) => `  - ${issue.path.join('.')}: ${issue.message}`)
50	      .join('\n');
51	
52	    throw new Error(
53	      `Missing or invalid client environment variables:\n${missing}`,
54	    );
55	  }
56	
57	  return result.data;
58	}
59	
60	// ── Exported validated env objects ──────────────────────────────────────
61	
62	/**
63	 * Server-only environment variables.
64	 * Throws at import time if any required variable is missing or invalid.
65	 * Throws if imported from a client component (browser context).
66	 */
67	export const serverEnv = validateServerEnv();
68	
69	/**
70	 * Client-safe environment variables (NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix only).
71	 * Safe to import from both server and client components.
72	 */
73	export const clientEnv = validateClientEnv();
74
```

23	async function seedAdmin(): Promise<void> {
24	  const email = process.env.ADMIN_EMAIL;
25	  const passwordHash = process.env.ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH;
26	
27	  if (!email || !passwordHash) {

29	      'Skipping admin seed: ADMIN_EMAIL or ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH not set.',
30	    );
31	    return;
32	  }
33	
34	  await prisma.user.upsert({
35	    where: { email },
36	    update: { passwordHash, nama: 'Admin' },
37	    create: { email, passwordHash, nama: 'Admin' },
38	  });
39	
40	  console.log(`Admin user upserted: ${email}`);
41	}

/**
 * _If you are looking to migrate from v4, visit the [Upgrade Guide (v5)](https://authjs.dev/getting-started/migrating-to-v5)._
 *
 * ## Installation
 *
 * ```bash npm2yarn
 * npm install next-auth@beta
 * ```
 *
 * ## Environment variable inference
 *
 * `NEXTAUTH_URL` and `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` have been inferred since v4.
 *
 * Since NextAuth.js v5 can also automatically infer environment variables that are prefixed with `AUTH_`.
 *
 * For example `AUTH_GITHUB_ID` and `AUTH_GITHUB_SECRET` will be used as the `clientId` and `clientSecret` options for the GitHub provider.
 *
 * :::tip
 * The environment variable name inferring has the following format for OAuth providers: `AUTH_{PROVIDER}_{ID|SECRET}`.
 *
 * `PROVIDER` is the uppercase snake case version of the provider's id, followed by either `ID` or `SECRET` respectively.
 * :::
 *
 * `AUTH_SECRET` and `AUTH_URL` are also aliased for `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` and `NEXTAUTH_URL` for consistency.
 *
 * To add social login to your app, the configuration becomes:
 *
 * ```ts title="auth.ts"
 * import NextAuth from "next-auth"
 * import GitHub from "next-auth/providers/github"
 * export const { handlers, auth } = NextAuth({ providers: [ GitHub ] })
 * ```
 *
 * And the `.env.local` file:
 *
 * ```sh title=".env.local"
 * AUTH_GITHUB_ID=...
 * AUTH_GITHUB_SECRET=...
 * AUTH_SECRET=...
 * ```
 *
 * :::tip
 * In production, `AUTH_SECRET` is a required environment variable - if not set, NextAuth.js will throw an error. See [MissingSecretError](https://authjs.dev/reference/core/errors#missingsecret) for more details.
 * :::
 *
 * If you need to override the default values for a provider, you can still call it as a function `GitHub({...})` as before.
 *
 * ## Lazy initialization
 * You can also initialize NextAuth.js lazily (previously known as advanced intialization), which allows you to access the request context in the configuration in some cases, like Route Handlers, Middleware, API Routes or `getServerSideProps`.
 * The above example becomes:
```

```typescript
import NextAuth from 'next-auth';
import Credentials from 'next-auth/providers/credentials';
import { compare } from 'bcryptjs';
import { prisma } from '@/lib/db';

declare module 'next-auth' {
  interface Session {
    user: {
      id: string;
      nama: string;
      email: string;
    };
  }

  interface User {
    nama: string;
  }
}

declare module '@auth/core/jwt' {
  interface JWT {
    id: string;
    nama: string;
  }
}

export const { handlers, auth, signIn, signOut } = NextAuth({
  secret: process.env.NEXTAUTH_SECRET,
  session: {
    strategy: 'jwt',
    maxAge: 24 * 60 * 60, // 24 hours
  },
  pages: {
    signIn: '/admin/login',
  },
  providers: [
    Credentials({
      name: 'credentials',
      credentials: {
        email: { label: 'E-mel', type: 'email' },
        password: { label: 'Kata Laluan', type: 'password' },
      },
      async authorize(credentials) {
        const email =
          typeof credentials?.email === 'string' ? credentials.email : '';
        const password =
          typeof credentials?.password === 'string'
            ? credentials.password
            : '';

        if (!email || !password) {
          throw new Error('E-mel atau kata laluan tidak sah');
        }

        const user = await prisma.user.findUnique({
          where: { email },
        });

        if (!user) {
          throw new Error('E-mel atau kata laluan tidak sah');
        }

        const isPasswordValid = await compare(password, user.passwordHash);

        if (!isPasswordValid) {
          throw new Error('E-mel atau kata laluan tidak sah');
        }

        return {
          id: user.id,
          email: user.email,
          nama: user.nama,
        };
      },
    }),
  ],
  callbacks: {
    jwt({ token, user }) {
      if (user) {
        token.id = user.id as string;
        token.nama = (user as { nama: string }).nama;
      }
      return token;
    },
    session({ session, token }) {
      return {
        ...session,
        user: {
          ...session.user,
          id: token.id,
          nama: token.nama,
        },
      };
    },
  },
});

```

```typescript
import { auth } from '@/lib/auth';
import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';

export default auth((req) => {
  const { pathname } = req.nextUrl;

  // Allow access to the login page without authentication
  if (pathname === '/admin/login') {
    return NextResponse.next();
  }

  // Protect all other /admin/* routes
  if (!req.auth) {
    const loginUrl = new URL('/admin/login', req.nextUrl.origin);
    loginUrl.searchParams.set('callbackUrl', pathname);
    return NextResponse.redirect(loginUrl);
  }

  return NextResponse.next();
});

export const config = {
  matcher: ['/admin/:path*'],
};

```

const inter = Inter({
  variable: "--font-inter",
  subsets: ["latin"],
  display: "swap",
});

  variable: "--font-playfair-display",
  subsets: ["latin"],
  display: "swap",
});

  title: "MawarBiru - Wedding Venue",

}: Readonly<{

}>) {

      lang="ms"

    >
      <body className="min-h-full flex flex-col bg-cream text-dark">
        {children}
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}

```tsx
'use client';

import { forwardRef, type ButtonHTMLAttributes } from 'react';
import { cva, type VariantProps } from 'class-variance-authority';
import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';

const buttonVariants = cva(
  [
    'inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2',
    'font-sans font-medium',
    'rounded-button',
    'transition-colors duration-150',
    'motion-reduce:transition-none',
    'focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2 focus-visible:ring-dusty-rose',
    'active:scale-[0.98]',
    'disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50',
  ],
  {
    variants: {
      variant: {
        primary: 'bg-navy text-cream hover:bg-navy-light active:bg-navy-dark',
        secondary:
          'bg-cream text-navy border border-navy hover:bg-cream-dark active:bg-cream-dark',
        accent:
          'bg-dusty-rose text-navy hover:bg-dusty-rose-light active:bg-dusty-rose-dark',
        ghost: 'bg-transparent text-navy hover:bg-cream-dark active:bg-cream-dark',
        danger: 'bg-red-600 text-white hover:bg-red-700 active:bg-red-800',
      },
      size: {
        sm: 'h-8 px-3 text-sm',
        md: 'h-10 px-4 text-base',
        lg: 'h-12 px-6 text-lg',
      },
    },
    defaultVariants: {
      variant: 'primary',
      size: 'md',
    },
  },
);

type ButtonProps = ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement> &
  VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {
    loading?: boolean;
    'data-testid'?: string;
  };

const Button = forwardRef<HTMLButtonElement, ButtonProps>(
  (
    {
      className,
      variant,
      size,
      loading = false,
      disabled,
      children,
      ...props
    },
    ref,
  ) => {
    return (
      <button
        ref={ref}
        className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size }), className)}
        disabled={disabled || loading}
        aria-busy={loading || undefined}
        {...props}
      >
        {loading && <Spinner />}
        {children}
      </button>
    );
  },
);

'use client';

import { forwardRef, useId, type InputHTMLAttributes } from 'react';
import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';
import { Label } from './label';

type InputProps = InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement> & {
  label?: string;
  error?: string;
  helperText?: string;
  'data-testid'?: string;
};

const Input = forwardRef<HTMLInputElement, InputProps>(
  ({ className, label, error, helperText, id, required, ...props }, ref) => {
    const generatedId = useId();
    const inputId = id ?? generatedId;
    const errorId = error ? `${inputId}-error` : undefined;
    const helperId = helperText ? `${inputId}-helper` : undefined;
    const describedBy = [errorId, helperId].filter(Boolean).join(' ') || undefined;

    return (
      <div className="flex flex-col gap-1.5">
        {label && (
          <Label htmlFor={inputId} required={required}>
            {label}
          </Label>
        )}
        <input
          ref={ref}
          id={inputId}
          required={required}
          aria-invalid={error ? true : undefined}
          aria-describedby={describedBy}
          className={cn(
            'rounded-button border bg-cream px-3 py-2 text-base text-navy',
            'transition-colors duration-150',
            'motion-reduce:transition-none',
            'placeholder:text-navy/50',
            'focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-dusty-rose focus-visible:ring-offset-2',
            'disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50',
            error
              ? 'border-red-600 focus-visible:ring-red-600'
              : 'border-navy',
            className,
          )}
          {...props}
        />
        {error && (
          <p id={errorId} className="text-sm text-red-600" role="alert">
            {error}
          </p>
        )}
        {helperText && !error && (
          <p id={helperId} className="text-sm text-navy/60">
            {helperText}
          </p>
        )}
      </div>
    );
  },
);

Input.displayName = 'Input';

export { Input };
export type { InputProps };

```

'use client';

import {
  forwardRef,
  useId,
  useCallback,
  type TextareaHTMLAttributes,
} from 'react';
import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';
import { Label } from './label';

type TextareaProps = TextareaHTMLAttributes<HTMLTextAreaElement> & {
  label?: string;
  error?: string;
  helperText?: string;
  autoResize?: boolean;
  'data-testid'?: string;
};

const Textarea = forwardRef<HTMLTextAreaElement, TextareaProps>(
  (
    {
      className,
      label,
      error,
      helperText,
      autoResize = false,
      id,
      required,
      onInput,
      ...props
    },
    ref,
  ) => {
    const generatedId = useId();
    const textareaId = id ?? generatedId;
    const errorId = error ? `${textareaId}-error` : undefined;
    const helperId = helperText ? `${textareaId}-helper` : undefined;
    const describedBy =
      [errorId, helperId].filter(Boolean).join(' ') || undefined;

    const handleInput = useCallback(
      (e: React.FormEvent<HTMLTextAreaElement>) => {
        if (autoResize) {
          const target = e.currentTarget;
          target.style.height = 'auto';
          target.style.height = `${target.scrollHeight}px`;
        }
        onInput?.(e);
      },
      [autoResize, onInput],
    );

    return (
      <div className="flex flex-col gap-1.5">
        {label && (
          <Label htmlFor={textareaId} required={required}>
            {label}
          </Label>
        )}
        <textarea
          ref={ref}
          id={textareaId}
          required={required}
          aria-invalid={error ? true : undefined}
          aria-describedby={describedBy}
          onInput={handleInput}
          className={cn(
            'min-h-[5rem] rounded-button border bg-cream px-3 py-2 text-base text-navy',
            'transition-colors duration-150',
            'motion-reduce:transition-none',
            'placeholder:text-navy/50',
            'focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-dusty-rose focus-visible:ring-offset-2',
            'disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50',
            autoResize && 'resize-none overflow-hidden',
            error
              ? 'border-red-600 focus-visible:ring-red-600'
              : 'border-navy',
            className,
          )}
          {...props}
        />
        {error && (
          <p id={errorId} className="text-sm text-red-600" role="alert">
            {error}
          </p>
        )}
        {helperText && !error && (
          <p id={helperId} className="text-sm text-navy/60">
            {helperText}
          </p>
        )}
      </div>
    );
  },
);

Textarea.displayName = 'Textarea';

export { Textarea };
export type { TextareaProps };

```

import { forwardRef, type HTMLAttributes } from 'react';
import { cva, type VariantProps } from 'class-variance-authority';
import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';

const badgeVariants = cva(
  [
    'inline-flex items-center',
    'rounded-badge px-2.5 py-0.5',
    'text-xs font-medium',
    'transition-colors duration-150',
    'motion-reduce:transition-none',
  ],
  {
    variants: {
      variant: {
        default: 'bg-navy text-cream',
        success: 'bg-green-600 text-white',
        warning: 'bg-amber-500 text-navy',
        error: 'bg-red-600 text-white',
        custom: '',
      },
    },
    defaultVariants: {
      variant: 'default',
    },
  },
);

type BadgeProps = HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement> &
  VariantProps<typeof badgeVariants> & {
    'data-testid'?: string;
  };

const Badge = forwardRef<HTMLSpanElement, BadgeProps>(
  ({ className, variant, style, ...props }, ref) => {
    return (
      <span
        ref={ref}
        className={cn(badgeVariants({ variant }), className)}
        style={style}
        {...props}
      />
    );
  },
);

Badge.displayName = 'Badge';

export { Badge, badgeVariants };
export type { BadgeProps };

import { forwardRef, type HTMLAttributes } from 'react';
import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';

/* ─── Card Root ─── */

type CardProps = HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> & {
  hoverLift?: boolean;
  'data-testid'?: string;
};

const Card = forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, CardProps>(
  ({ className, hoverLift = false, ...props }, ref) => {
    return (
      <div
        ref={ref}
        className={cn(
          'rounded-card bg-cream shadow-card',
          'transition-shadow duration-150',
          'motion-reduce:transition-none',
          hoverLift && 'hover:shadow-elevated',
          className,
        )}
        {...props}
      />
    );
  },
);

Card.displayName = 'Card';

/* ─── Card Header ─── */

type CardHeaderProps = HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> & {
  'data-testid'?: string;
};

const CardHeader = forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, CardHeaderProps>(
  ({ className, ...props }, ref) => {
    return (
      <div
        ref={ref}
        className={cn('px-6 pt-6 pb-0', className)}
        {...props}
      />
    );
  },
);

CardHeader.displayName = 'CardHeader';

/* ─── Card Body ─── */

type CardBodyProps = HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> & {
  'data-testid'?: string;
};

const CardBody = forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, CardBodyProps>(
  ({ className, ...props }, ref) => {
    return (
      <div ref={ref} className={cn('px-6 py-4', className)} {...props} />
    );
  },
);

CardBody.displayName = 'CardBody';

/* ─── Card Footer ─── */

type CardFooterProps = HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> & {
  'data-testid'?: string;
};

const CardFooter = forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, CardFooterProps>(
  ({ className, ...props }, ref) => {
    return (
      <div
        ref={ref}
        className={cn('px-6 pt-0 pb-6', className)}
        {...props}
      />
    );
  },
);

CardFooter.displayName = 'CardFooter';

/* ─── Compound export ─── */

const CardNamespace = Object.assign(Card, {
  Header: CardHeader,
  Body: CardBody,
  Footer: CardFooter,
});

export { CardNamespace as Card, CardHeader, CardBody, CardFooter };
export type { CardProps, CardHeaderProps, CardBodyProps, CardFooterProps };

11	    'rounded-button',
12	    'transition-colors duration-150',
